Hello @DaKyzen , can you please explain how to correctly setup this node or maybe share some exemplary workflow?
Also this isn't n8n trigger node, the name is misleading, you should rename it or, if it possible, change it to trigger node, here some information that can be useful https://docs.n8n.io/nodes/creating-nodes/create-trigger-node.html#prerequisites
author: michael-radency

body:
Hello @michael-radency,

The node is for detecting changes to a MongoDb collection and outputting the change details.

Setting up the node is the same as the current MongoDb node in terms of entering the credentials. As for the parameters, there's only the collection field and the Full Json. The collection field is for specifying what collection the node should be watching, while the full documnet switch specifies whether or not to show only the fields that have changed or all the fields of the record. Hopefully the two screenshots make things clearer.

![Sample workflow](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/73026342/158066224-b7fed701-19f3-4e82-9319-90b79afee38c.PNG)

![Table](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/73026342/158066216-caaad5a4-315b-4d4c-9a44-08cfc8b0200d.PNG)

As for the node not being a trigger node, can you elaborate further? I believe that I followed the instructions on creating a trigger node from your link. There was a bug that I just noticed where my trigger node did not appear under the trigger section in the node list which I fixed. I've also updated the name to be more specific to change streams.
author: DaKyzen

body:
Hey @m2scared,

I like the idea, I am not sure if "Respond to trigger" is too generic though as it wouldn't do anything for any of the other trigger nodes at the moment (apart from webhooks).

Would it maybe be better as an option in the RabbitMQ node for now if possible? Do you also have an example of when you may use this?
author: Joffcom

body:
> Hey @m2scared,

Hi @Joffcom,
 
> I like the idea, I am not sure if "Respond to trigger" is too generic though as it wouldn't do anything for any of the other trigger nodes at the moment (apart from webhooks).

Understood. I used this name because it can be reused in other "queue" nodes that support NACK such as Kafka.

> Would it maybe be better as an option in the RabbitMQ node for now if possible? Do you also have an example of when you may use this?

I agree. My use case is: the event should be acknowledge only if the workflow succeeds. Currently this is not possible with n8n as far as I know. Is there any other way to archive this?

I will change the patch only for RabbitMQ.
author: m2scared

body: No other way I can think of to handle it at the moment, Makes sense that you would only want to ack a success as well. In the past when implementing a logging system for file transfers with an MQ broker (Websphere I think it was) we would just post a sucess message to a queue / topic and include the original MQ ID as a header but I guess something like that would depend on how the interacting systems are working together.
author: Joffcom

body:
> No other way I can think of to handle it at the moment, Makes sense that you would only want to ack a success as well. In the past when implementing a logging system for file transfers with an MQ broker (Websphere I think it was) we would just post a sucess message to a queue / topic and include the original MQ ID as a header but I guess something like that would depend on how the interacting systems are working together.

I am not quite sure if I understood. Do you have examples? Code?

It seems possible to do since there is a similar behavior with 'webhook' and 'respond to webhool'. My current implementation uses `createDeferredPromise` and works as expected. I don't know if it will work with worker n8n nodes.
author: m2scared

body: Sadly nothing I can share for the old MQ project as it was done using other systems for an old customer, If I get a chance I will see what I can remember but chances are as you are already doing something with MQ it may not be that helpful. It all depends on what your MQ process currently is, So we were getting an MQ message with transfer info in it and would then connect to a server to download or upload a file then anything that happened would be logged to a queue and the MQ ID was passed along the MQ messages. They then had this neat web based system which would show them everything when then entered the ID.
author: Joffcom

body:
Hello @m2scared , thank you for your contribution, as for now it seems that RespondToTrigger node is redundant and too tied to RabbitMQTrigger node, as this node only hold boolean value it probably can be replaced with an option in RabbitMQ trigger node. 

Would be great if you can share workflow with setup where you are using this node, as it will clarify use case and gives  possibility of alternative solution without necessity to add additional node.

RabbitMQTrigger uses amqplib, [here](https://amqp-node.github.io/amqplib/channel_api.html#channel_ack) is documentation that can be useful.

body:
@Joffcom
There is a small distinction between pdf-parse and pdfdataextract in the json structure returned.

| pdf-parse                | pdfdataextract |
|---------------------|----------------|
| numpages               | pages              |
| version                    | -                     |
| numrender              | -                     |
| -                             | fingerprint       |
| -                             | outline             |
| -                             | permissions      |
| text -> string(\n\n)  | text -> string[] |

so if we merge it now, we have a "breaking change".
The question is what is wanted, a "breaking change" or that the structure is adapted to the old version.

```js
{
  numpages : pages,
  version: '2.10.377',
  numrender: text.length,
  fingerprint: fingerprint,
  outline: outline,
  permissions: permissions,
  text: text.join('\n\n')
}
```

It is a bit of a tricky one, At the moment we are prioritizing requests that improve / fix issues with existing nodes. Once we are at a better number with those we can then start working through the new nodes , I know there are about 50 new node PRs waiting.

body:
Hmm it looks like my `filter` and `sort` options from my original PR were dropped from the `submission:getAll` operation, this seriously limits the usefulness of the node because I believe it will be extremely rare that users will want to retrieve all submissions in a call (which is why I felt that `query` was a better name than `getAll` - though I understand the objective of consistency) - typical surveys will have 10s of thousands of submissions (ours have millions).

I can submit a new PR to add them back in, but before I invest time into this I'd like to know if there was any specific reasons those were dropped @RicardoE105 ?

Also, I'm wondering about the attachment naming. My PR had logic to name the files after the corresponding question in the survey, which I think will be the most common case (and is guaranteed not to have conflicts). It looks like this was also dropped. This is probably less crucial, but this is certainly less convenient that way... Shall I add this back in as well?

body:
Even though adding the parent id solves the issue for almost all cases, I would still like the option to return the blocks as array.
1. There is no "group by key" node so I would need to add a function to do this each time I want to regroup blocks per page.
2. If there are 2 calls to the same page, it would be even harder to regroup the items per call.

So in my case I would continue using the http request node instead since it makes my life easier.
author: knshiro

body: @knshiro I totally see where you are coming from and totally agree that the current solution is not perfect. That is however consistent with how it works with other nodes in n8n. We definitely also have to make possible what you need, but that should not happen on a node-level. Because else we would have to add similar logic to every n8n node. To avoid that, would it be better to find a proper global solution instead that works everywhere.
author: janober
knshiro

body: It's very a valid problem: my preferred way of dealing with it would be to add a 'group by' operation to the 'item lists' node.
author: sirdavidoff

body:
I completely agree with adding the group by feature. I even already gave that feedback because I implemented it for something else.

One last try about why this is case is different:
Even though the endpoint is called get blocks, what it actually is "get page content" and I think we should not get stuck in the semantics but focus on actual use cases. 
So while your flow is to only work one page, treating the content of one page as multiple items can be useful to do things like actions per checklist items for example. If you treat multiple pages you want to get those page contents treated as that.
My use case for example, is to create a basic html rendering for each page I fetch. In that case, the block for me is just a part of the page, it's not an item I want to treat individually (e.g. to produce a `<ul>` I need to be aware of all the `bullet_list_items` in order to group them).

I have not seen any new nodes drop into the review pile yet, there are still a few node improvements / fixes to run through but we are moving fairly quickly on them.

Is the client currently using the node ok with it running as a custom node?
author: Joffcom

body:
Hey @Joffcom,
Well the story is, my client's client wants to continue using n8n.cloud without dealing with the technicalities of setting up a custom node, so they are happy to wait for the official rollout of the Datagma node. I'll pass your message along and say your team is still in the process of node improvements, thanks for your help 👍
author: d4vidsha
Joffcom

body:
Hello again @Joffcom,
is there anything I can do to bring this all together? Like writing authentication docs if necessary? I see that most nodes have one of these and just want to do the best I can to get it all completed smoothly. Planning on running through the checklist soon as another measure.
author: d4vidsha

body:
Hi @d4vidsha, niv from n8n's product team here
First of all, thanks for the contribution, highly appreciated
At the moment, a lot of eng efforts are dedicated to improving existing nodes rather than creating new ones
This happens for 2 reasons
1. We still have a lot of work to do improving the existing nodes to provide the best experience for those who already use n8n
2. One of the upcoming features is a community nodes repository, where users can upload their node (and node packages) to npmjs repo, and with a simple UI, load those into their n8n instance. We don't have yet a set in stone release date for this, but it should happen rather soon. One open question is if this functionality would be available for cloud users also. It looks like the answer for this is "yes" but it's not set in stone as well.
Once we would make the final decision on this, and once the feature is released, we could make better estimations to when we could process new nodes contributions (if needed at all)

In the meantime, have you tried using the HTTPRequest node for that? It's not as good an experience as a native node, but it eventually does the work (we also have plans on our short term roadmap of improving the experience on that front)
author: nivbe06

body:
Hey @d4vidsha,

You could pop the docs together if you wanted to, to be honest though while it will make life easier when / if we merge the node it won't move it up the list yet.

We still have a few older PRs to look at before we start to tackle new nodes then it will be handled from oldest to newest with the most requested by the community being handled first.

I have been chatting to Raphael about this one as well.
author: Joffcom

body: Thank you all for this info!
author: d4vidsha

body:
Hey @d4vidsha,

Thanks for the contribution, It looks like you have already published this node to the Community Node Repository which is our preferred method for new node submissions.

In the future we may reach out to you about including your node in our main release, But for now I am going to close this PR. If you have any questions about this or need any help let us know.
